questions.question: What is the primary reason AlexNet used the ReLU activation function instead of sigmoid/tanh? (2 marks)
expectedPoints: ReLU helps mitigate the vanishing gradient problem
It allows for faster training convergence compared to saturating activation functions

questions.question: Compare the LeNet-5 and AlexNet architectures. Discuss at least four key differences in their design and implementation. (5 marks)
expectedPoints: Scale: LeNet-5 was small (~60k params) for digits, AlexNet was large (~62M params) for ImageNet
Depth: AlexNet has more convolutional layers (5 vs 3)
Activation: AlexNet used ReLU; LeNet-5 used tanh/sigmoid
Pooling: AlexNet used max pooling (often overlapping); LeNet-5 used average pooling
Regularization: AlexNet employed dropout and data augmentation; LeNet-5 did not
Hardware: AlexNet was trained on GPUs; LeNet-5 on CPUs
